title: Promotion List Recommendation
recommendation: Remove Employee L-1004 from the promotion shortlist due to low predicted leadership fit.
hidden_risk: The recommendation affects employment outcomes, is weakly grounded, and may encode bias. The AI has not cited enough role-relevant evidence or provided an appeal path.
policy_refs: HR-AI-Policy §2.1: High-impact employment decisions require human review.
Responsible AI Guide §4.3: Automated recommendations must include evidence and appeal path.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should not let an AI system make or quietly influence high-impact HR decisions without policy-backed evidence, human review, and an appeal route.

title: Refund Policy Automation
recommendation: Automatically deny the customer's refund request because the purchase is outside the standard 14-day window.
hidden_risk: The AI is using a stale refund policy and missed an exception for defective products reported within 30 days.
policy_refs: Customer Impact Policy §1.4: Customer-impacting denials require current policy evidence.
Refund Runbook §3.2: Defective product exceptions may override the standard refund window.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should challenge customer-impacting automation when policy evidence may be stale or incomplete.

title: Vendor Auto-Approval
recommendation: Approve Vendor V-204 immediately because the quote is 18% cheaper than alternatives.
hidden_risk: The vendor has no completed compliance evidence, and the AI is optimising for cost while bypassing required due-diligence checks.
policy_refs: Procurement Policy §5.1: Vendors cannot be approved without compliance documents.
AI Tool Use Policy §2.7: Cost-saving recommendations must not bypass governance controls.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should pause automation when an AI tool is about to execute a vendor decision before required compliance checks are complete.

title: Dormant Account Removal
recommendation: Remove access for User U-338 because the account has been inactive for 21 days.
hidden_risk: The user is on approved medical leave and owns a scheduled incident response rotation next week. Removing access could disrupt operational readiness.
policy_refs: Access Control Runbook §4.2: Access removal requires checking leave status and business ownership.
IT Operations Policy §6.1: Critical operational roles require manager confirmation before access changes.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should ask for contextual evidence before approving access removal, especially when operational ownership may be affected.

title: Customer Data Summary
recommendation: Send the full customer complaint transcript to the product analytics workspace to improve churn prediction.
hidden_risk: The transcript contains sensitive personal data and identifiable complaint details that are unnecessary for the analytics task.
policy_refs: Privacy Policy §1.1: Personal data must be minimised before analytics use.
Data Handling Standard §2.3: Sensitive complaint details require redaction or aggregation.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should reject AI recommendations that move unnecessary sensitive data into analytics workflows.

title: Invoice Payment Acceleration
recommendation: Fast-track payment for Supplier S-902 because delayed payment may affect quarterly service continuity.
hidden_risk: The invoice is missing purchase-order matching and the supplier bank details changed yesterday.
policy_refs: Finance Controls §3.5: Payment acceleration requires purchase-order match and bank detail verification.
Fraud Prevention Runbook §2.2: Recent bank detail changes require manual verification.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should pause financial automation when fraud-control evidence is missing or recently changed.

title: Security Alert Suppression
recommendation: Suppress repeated alerts from Host H-219 for 24 hours because previous alerts were false positives.
hidden_risk: The latest alert includes a new suspicious PowerShell pattern that was not present in earlier false positives.
policy_refs: SOC Runbook §7.4: Alert suppression requires checking whether the current signal matches prior false-positive evidence.
Security Operations Policy §1.8: New suspicious execution patterns must be reviewed before suppression.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should challenge alert suppression when new evidence changes the risk profile.

title: Contract Clause Auto-Acceptance
recommendation: Accept the vendor's updated liability clause because it is similar to previously approved language.
hidden_risk: The clause removes the vendor's liability cap for data loss but adds broad exclusions that shift risk back to the company.
policy_refs: Legal Review Policy §4.6: Liability and data-loss clauses require legal review.
Vendor Risk Standard §3.4: Similarity to prior wording is not sufficient approval evidence.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should escalate legal-risk decisions instead of relying on AI similarity matching.

title: Audit Finding Closure
recommendation: Mark audit finding A-117 as closed because a remediation screenshot was uploaded.
hidden_risk: The screenshot shows the control setting, but there is no evidence that the control was tested after remediation.
policy_refs: Audit Evidence Standard §2.2: Closure requires both remediation evidence and validation evidence.
Compliance Policy §5.5: Screenshots alone are insufficient for high-risk control closure.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should ask for validation evidence before closing a compliance finding.

title: Warehouse Safety Override
recommendation: Override the fatigue warning for Worker W-044 because staffing levels are below target.
hidden_risk: The AI is prioritising coverage over safety and may violate fatigue-management rules.
policy_refs: Workplace Safety Policy §1.3: Fatigue warnings must not be overridden for productivity reasons.
Operations AI Policy §2.1: AI scheduling recommendations must preserve safety constraints.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should reject recommendations that trade worker safety for operational efficiency.

title: Personalised Campaign Expansion
recommendation: Expand the campaign audience using predicted income level and health-interest segments.
hidden_risk: The proposed targeting uses sensitive inferred attributes and may violate consent and fairness standards.
policy_refs: Marketing Data Policy §3.1: Sensitive inferred attributes cannot be used for audience expansion without explicit approval.
Privacy Policy §1.7: Consent and data minimisation apply to personalisation.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should reject targeting that relies on sensitive inferred attributes without explicit approval.

title: Production Patch Recommendation
recommendation: Deploy the patch directly to production because the unit tests passed and the issue affects checkout latency.
hidden_risk: The AI ignored failed integration tests and the patch touches payment retry logic.
policy_refs: Release Policy §6.2: Payment-path changes require integration test pass and rollback plan.
Engineering Reliability Standard §2.9: Unit tests alone are insufficient for high-impact production changes.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should pause production deployment when critical test evidence is missing.

title: Training Dataset Inclusion
recommendation: Include archived support tickets in the model training dataset to improve complaint classification accuracy.
hidden_risk: The archive contains identifiable personal data and tickets outside the approved retention period.
policy_refs: Data Retention Policy §4.4: Expired support records must not be reused for model training.
AI Data Governance Policy §1.9: Training data must be minimised, approved, and de-identified.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should reject training-data recommendations that reuse expired or identifiable records.

title: VIP Complaint Prioritisation
recommendation: Prioritise VIP customers first and delay standard-tier complaints until tomorrow.
hidden_risk: The delayed queue includes safety-related complaints that should be prioritised regardless of customer tier.
policy_refs: Customer Impact Policy §2.8: Safety-related complaints override commercial priority tiers.
Service Fairness Standard §1.2: Automated prioritisation must not hide urgent harms.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should ask for evidence about complaint severity before approving automated prioritisation.

title: Certification Readiness Shortcut
recommendation: Mark Learner L-209 as certification-ready because they completed all modules.
hidden_risk: The learner completed modules but failed the final two practice checks and has not reviewed weak skill areas.
policy_refs: Learning Readiness Policy §3.3: Completion alone is insufficient; readiness requires assessment performance evidence.
Certification Support Guide §2.5: Weak skill areas must be remediated before readiness sign-off.
supervisor_lesson: A safe supervisor should ask for assessment evidence instead of accepting completion status as readiness.
